payloads.data: If I receive a gift from a colleague at another central bank, do I need to register it with anyone? Please state whether registration is required, any value thresholds or exceptions, and which office or register (if any) I should notify and how.

args: If I receive a gift from a colleague at another central bank, do I need to register it with anyone? Please state whether registration is required, any value thresholds or exceptions, and which office or register (if any) I should notify and how.

the same source, the following may be accepted: 
(a) hospitality offered by the private sector with a value of up to EUR 50 if it is 
offered in the context of a work -related occasion. This exception shall not 
apply to hospitality offered by current or potential suppliers or by credit

0.2.2 Gifts and hospitality 
0.2.2.1 Members of staff may neither solicit nor accept for themselves or any other person 
any advantage connected in any way with their employment with the ECB.  
0.2.2.2 An ‘advantage’ is any gift, hospitality or other benefit of a financial or non-financial 
nature which objectively improves the financial, legal or personal situation of the 
recipient or any other person and to which the recipient is not entitled by  law. 
Minor hospitality offered during a work-related meeting shall not be considered as 
an advantage. An ‘advantage’ is considered connected with a recipient’s 
employment with the ECB if it is offered on the basis of the recipient’s position 
with the ECB, rather than on a personal basis.

Members of staff shall hand over to the ECB any gifts accepted in ac cordance 
with Article 0.2.2.3(c). Such gifts shall become the ECB’s property.

0.4.2.4 Private financial transactions subject to ex-post reporting 
Members of staff shall report to the Compliance and Governance Office any 
private financial transaction exceeding EUR 10 000 within any given calendar 
month which does not fall under one of the previous three categories within 30 
calendar days after its ex ecution. The Compliance and Governance Office shall 
define the information to be reported, the reporting format and the procedure.  
The obligation to report shall apply in particular to: 
(a) loans other than mortgages (including switching from a fixed to a floating 
arrangement, or vice- versa, or extending an existing loan). Members of 
staff shall indicate whether the loan is used for the acquisition of financial 
instruments; 
(b) interest rate-related derivatives and derivatives based on indices;  
(c) purchases or sales of shares of corporations other than the ones set out in

messages.content:
Your input fields are:
1. `context` (list[str]): 
2. `question` (str):
Your output fields are:
1. `reasoning` (str): 
2. `answer` (str):
All interactions will be structured in the following way, with the appropriate values filled in.

[[ ## context ## ]]
{context}

[[ ## question ## ]]
{question}

[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
{reasoning}

[[ ## answer ## ]]
{answer}

[[ ## completed ## ]]
In adhering to this structure, your objective is: 
        You are an ECB HR policy assistant. Use only the provided Context to answer. Produce two fields: 
        - Reasoning: a step-by-step synthesis of the relevant provisions you will use (identify the main rule, any exceptions, competent authorities, timelines, figures/thresholds, and the exact page numbers you will cite; reconcile overlaps/conflicts by preferring the most specific and most recent provision and noting this).
        - Answer: a concise, impersonal, normative response grounded in the Context.
        
        Answer requirements:
        - Lead with the main entitlement/obligation/rule that directly addresses the question.
        - State precise figures, thresholds and formats as in source (e.g., EUR 764; six months; one month’s notice), and spell in British/European English (hyphenate terms like fixed-term; use ECB terminology).
        - Make timelines explicit (start triggers, end conditions, cut-offs) and name the competent authority and their role when relevant (e.g., Chief Services Officer, on behalf of the Executive Board; Director General Human Resources or their Deputy).
        - If the question has multiple parts, answer sequentially with numbered points (1., 2., …), stating defaults and consequences for each.
        - End the Answer with parenthetical citations to every source used, giving exact page numbers and, where available, article/section markers. Use the documents’ canonical names: “ECB Staff Rules” and “ECB Conditions of Employment” (e.g., (ECB Staff Rules, §2.1.1–2.1.2, p.47; ECB Conditions of Employment, p.6)).
        - Do not invent content or cite pages not present in the Context. If the Context is insufficient to answer reliably or lacks the precise page needed, provide a neutral fallback: “Insufficient retrieved source detail to answer reliably. Please provide the relevant provision and page number(s) from the ECB Staff Rules or Conditions of Employment.” Do not add citations in this case.
        
        Style:
        - Impersonal, concise, and prescriptive; avoid first person. Prefer “Members of staff/applicants/appointments are …” over “you,” unless the question explicitly asks about the user’s situation and the rule is universal.
        - Avoid long quotations; summarise the rule precisely. 
        - Keep the Answer self-contained and limited to what is supported by the cited pages.

This is an example of the task, though some input or output fields are not supplied.

[[ ## question ## ]]
I want to do a transaction in euro-area government bonds worth EUR 15,000 in a single month. Do I need authorisation, and from whom, and how long before the order should I submit the request?

[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
Not supplied for this particular example. 

[[ ## answer ## ]]
Transactions exceeding EUR 10,000 within any given calendar month in government securities issued by euro area Member States are subject to prior authorisation by the Compliance and Governance Office. Any such request must be submitted at least five working days prior to the envisaged order date, and the Office shall decide on the request within five working days; if it does not react within five working days, the transaction is deemed authorised. (ECB Staff Rules, pages 15–17)

[[ ## completed ## ]]

This is an example of the task, though some input or output fields are not supplied.

[[ ## question ## ]]
How long is the cooling-off period for staff at salary band I or above who were involved in supervising a credit institution before they can join that institution?

[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
Not supplied for this particular example. 

[[ ## answer ## ]]
Members of staff at salary band I or above who were directly involved in the supervision of a credit institution may only start working for that credit institution after the expiry of one year from the date on which their direct involvement in the supervision ceased. In exceptional circumstances, this period may be increased up to two years. (ECB Staff Rules, page 10)

6.3.9 No reimbursement of expenses, indemnity or allowance under Article 6.3.7 shall 
be payable to a member of staff or those entitled under them whose accident or 
occupational disease arose from a situation in which they intentionally caused or 
contributed to the death or injury of another person.  
6.3.10 (a) Members of staff who suffer an alleged accident at work or those entitled 
under them shall report it to the Directorate General Human Resources. 
Where the alleged accident at work results in death or where it is impossible

done by any member of their family or any other person with knowledge of 
the facts.   
The report shall specify the date and time, the causes and the 
circumstances of the a"]

[[ ## question ## ]]
Within what timeframe must an alleged accident at work be reported to the Directorate General Human Resources?

[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
The provided excerpt from the ECB Staff Rules (page 112) states the reporting deadline: "The report shall be submitted not later than 10 working days following the date on which the alleged accident at work occurs except where the Director General Human Resources or their Deputy has established that the delay was justified by force majeure or by any other legitimate reason."

[[ ## answer ## ]]
An alleged accident at work must be reported to the Directorate General Human Resources not later than 10 working days following the date on which the accident occurs, unless the Director General Human Resources or their Deputy accepts that the delay was justified by force majeure or another legitimate reason.

[[ ## question ## ]]
What is the standard subsistence allowance amount and for how long can I receive it?

[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
The information is in the ECB Staff Rules excerpt (pages 74–75): Article 4.5.2 defines the standard subsistence allowance and the supplement; Article 4.5.1/4.5.3 and surrounding text state the maximum entitlement period.

[[ ## answer ## ]]
The standard subsistence allowance is EUR 9,270. The maximum period of entitlement to the subsistence allowance is three months (the allowance accrues on a daily basis). A supplement of up to EUR 2,070 may be added for a spouse/recognised partner and dependent child accompanying the staff member.

[[ ## question ## ]]
If I receive a gift from a colleague at another central bank, do I need to register it with anyone? Please state whether registration is required, any value thresholds or exceptions, and which office or register (if any) I should notify and how.

Respond with the corresponding output fields, starting with the field `[[ ## reasoning ## ]]`, then `[[ ## answer ## ]]`, and then ending with the marker for `[[ ## completed ## ]]`.

outputs:
[[ ## reasoning ## ]]
- Main rule: Members of staff may neither solicit nor accept any advantage connected with their employment; minor hospitality during work-related meetings is not an advantage (Article 0.2.2.1–0.2.2.2). Competent authority: Compliance and Governance Office. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)
- Exceptions: Article 0.2.2.3 permits, as exceptions (provided they are not frequent and not from the same source):  
  (a) private-sector hospitality up to EUR 50 in a work-related context (explicit monetary threshold);  
  (b) advantages offered by other central banks, national public bodies or European and international organisations which do not go beyond what is customary and considered appropriate (no monetary threshold set in the excerpt);  
  (c) advantages offered in specific circumstances where refusal would cause offence or put the professional relationship at serious risk. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)
- Registration obligation: Article 0.2.2.4 requires members of staff to register with the Compliance and Governance Office "without undue delay" in three cases:  
  (a) any advantage accepted under 0.2.2.3(c);  
  (b) any advantage whose value cannot be clearly established to be under the thresholds provided for in Article 0.2.2.3;  
  (c) any offer by third parties of advantages the acceptance of which is prohibited. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)
- Hand-over rule: Gifts accepted under 0.2.2.3(c) must be handed over to the ECB and become ECB property. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)
- Reconciliation for a gift from a colleague at another central bank: such an advantage falls under the exception in 0.2.2.3(b) if it "does not go beyond what is customary and considered appropriate" — in that case the exception allows acceptance and 0.2.2.4 does not automatically require registration. However, if the value cannot be clearly established to be under applicable thresholds (noting EUR 50 applies to the private-sector hospitality exception only) or the circumstances fall within 0.2.2.3(c), registration with the Compliance and Governance Office is required. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)
- Timelines/format: The rule uses the timeline "without undue delay" for registration. The provided excerpt does not specify the registration format or the precise procedural channel beyond naming the Compliance and Governance Office. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)

[[ ## answer ## ]]
1. Main rule: Gifts from another central bank may be accepted as an exception only if they "do not go beyond what is customary and considered appropriate"; such an exception is set out in Article 0.2.2.3(b). No automatic requirement to register arises from that exception alone. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)

2. Registration required (three situations): Register with the Compliance and Governance Office without undue delay if any of the following apply:  
   a. the gift was accepted under Article 0.2.2.3(c) (accepted because refusal would cause offence or put the professional relationship at serious risk);  
   b. the value of the advantage cannot be clearly established to be under the thresholds provided for in Article 0.2.2.3 (note: Article 0.2.2.3(a) contains the only explicit monetary threshold in the excerpt: EUR 50 for private-sector hospitality); or  
   c. the offer is from a third party of an advantage the acceptance of which is prohibited. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)

3. Office and timing: Notify/register with the Compliance and Governance Office "without undue delay". The excerpt does not specify the registration format or exact procedural steps beyond this requirement. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)

4. Hand-over: If the gift is accepted under Article 0.2.2.3(c), it must be handed over to the ECB and becomes ECB property. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)

(ECB Staff Rules, p.6)

[[ ## completed ## ]]

data.answer:
1. Main rule: Gifts from another central bank may be accepted as an exception only if they "do not go beyond what is customary and considered appropriate"; such an exception is set out in Article 0.2.2.3(b). No automatic requirement to register arises from that exception alone. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)

2. Registration required (three situations): Register with the Compliance and Governance Office without undue delay if any of the following apply:  
   a. the gift was accepted under Article 0.2.2.3(c) (accepted because refusal would cause offence or put the professional relationship at serious risk);  
   b. the value of the advantage cannot be clearly established to be under the thresholds provided for in Article 0.2.2.3 (note: Article 0.2.2.3(a) contains the only explicit monetary threshold in the excerpt: EUR 50 for private-sector hospitality); or  
   c. the offer is from a third party of an advantage the acceptance of which is prohibited. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)

3. Office and timing: Notify/register with the Compliance and Governance Office "without undue delay". The excerpt does not specify the registration format or exact procedural steps beyond this requirement. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)

4. Hand-over: If the gift is accepted under Article 0.2.2.3(c), it must be handed over to the ECB and becomes ECB property. (ECB Staff Rules, p.6)

(ECB Staff Rules, p.6)
